summary: The Blazor SKGLView component keeps its GRContext field private, unlike all other GPU-backed views (macOS, WPF, WinForms, WinUI, Metal) which expose it as a public property, preventing users from creating additional GPU surfaces sharing the same context.

stepsToReproduce: Use SKGLView in a Blazor WASM app
Attempt to access GRContext from the view to create an additional GPU surface
Observe that the context field is private and inaccessible

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The Blazor SKGLView still has a private context field with no public property in the current codebase.

analysis.summary: The Blazor SKGLView.razor.cs has a private GRContext field (line 24) with no public property, while every other GPU-backed view in SkiaSharp exposes GRContext publicly. Issue #358 added GRContext exposure to native views but the Blazor view (added later) was not given parity.
analysis.rationale: This is an enhancement request for API parity. The reporter correctly identifies that macOS SKGLView exposes GRContext publicly while the Blazor equivalent does not. All six other GPU view implementations (macOS SKGLView, macOS SKGLLayer, WPF SKGLElement, WinForms SKGLControl, WinUI SKSwapChainPanel, Apple SKMetalView) expose GRContext. The Blazor view is the only one missing it.

keySignals.text: On macOS we can directly access the GRContext of the view
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter is comparing Blazor to macOS view and found an inconsistency.

keySignals.text: In the Blazor view this is not an option since the field is private
keySignals.interpretation: Correctly identifies the private field as the gap.

keySignals.text: I'm using the GRContext of the view to generate an additional surface for rendering
keySignals.interpretation: Valid use case: sharing GPU context for offscreen rendering.

codeInvestigation.finding: GRContext is declared as `private GRContext? context;` with no public property exposing it.
WPF view exposes `public GRContext GRContext => grContext;` — confirms pattern used on other platforms.
WinUI view exposes `public GRContext GRContext => context;` — same pattern.
WinForms view exposes `public GRContext GRContext => grContext;` — same pattern.
macOS view exposes `public GRContext GRContext => context;` — the exact view the reporter references.
Metal view also exposes `public GRContext GRContext => context;` — universal pattern across all GPU views except Blazor.

workarounds: Access the GRContext indirectly via `e.Surface.Context` in the OnPaintSurface callback (returns GRRecordingContext, cast to GRContext if needed).

resolution.hypothesis: The Blazor SKGLView was implemented after issue #358 added GRContext exposure to native views, and the public property was simply omitted.

proposals.title: Add public GRContext property to Blazor SKGLView
proposals.description: Add `public GRContext? GRContext => context;` to SKGLView.razor.cs, matching the pattern used in all other GPU view implementations.

proposals.title: Use Surface.Context as workaround
proposals.description: Access the GRContext via `e.Surface.Context` inside the OnPaintSurface callback. This returns a GRRecordingContext which can be cast to GRContext. Limited to use within the paint callback only.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add public GRContext property to Blazor SKGLView
resolution.recommendedReason: Trivial one-line change that brings Blazor view to parity with all other GPU views. Matches existing pattern exactly.

actionability.reason: Valid enhancement request with trivial fix. All other GPU views expose GRContext; the Blazor view is the only exception.

actions.description: Apply enhancement, Blazor views, and WASM labels
Acknowledge the gap and provide workaround
Link to original GRContext exposure issue #358
